Markdown Content:
## AI Analysis

Claude / GPT-4o cached· cached

System Health

Degraded

Wake On-Call

No

Summary

The system is degraded but stable. The most notable issues are persistent FedEx rate API timeouts (15 errors, falling back to cached rates) and 20 Stripe charge latency SLA breaches, both of which could impact customer experience at checkout. Additionally, multiple SKUs are flagging low stock exclusively at warehouse-east, warranting an inventory replenishment check. No on-call page is required yet, but the FedEx and Stripe trends should be watched closely.

Flagged Clusters (3)

Medium cluster 0· shipping/rates

FedEx rate API is consistently timing out after ~5 seconds (15 occurrences), forcing fallback to cached rates. This suggests FedEx's external API is degraded or network connectivity to it is impaired. Cached rates may diverge from actual rates, potentially causing pricing inaccuracies for customers.

Medium cluster 1· payments/stripe

20 Stripe charges exceeded the SLA threshold (all ~930-970ms range), while successful charges complete in ~323-340ms. The consistent latency pattern near but under 1 second suggests Stripe is experiencing elevated response times or a specific payment flow is hitting a slow code path.

Medium cluster 3· inventory/warehouse-east

Multiple SKUs (at least 13 occurrences across clusters 3 and 2) are reporting low stock at warehouse-east specifically. This concentration at a single warehouse may indicate a replenishment failure or demand spike rather than isolated SKU issues.

Recommended Actions

1.

Investigate FedEx rate API connectivity: check network routes, FedEx status page, and assess how stale the cached rates are to determine customer pricing impact.

2.

Monitor Stripe p99 latency trend — if SLA breaches continue to climb, consider alerting Stripe support and reviewing whether a specific charge type or card network is the bottleneck.

3.

Trigger inventory replenishment review for warehouse-east; cross-check which SKUs are low and whether inbound stock is expected.

4.

Set up alerting for FedEx API timeout rate so on-call is paged if the fallback-to-cache pattern sustains beyond the current window.
